nameMeanings.name: Thornhill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name from any of various places called Thornhill, for example in Derbyshire, Yorkshire, Dorset, and Wiltshire, from Old English thorn ‘thorn tree’ + hyll ‘hill’.

variants.name: Thornell
meanings: Swedish: ornamental name composed of the element thorn, an ornamental spelling of torn ‘thorn bush’, + the suffix -ell, a derivative of the Latin adjectival ending -elius.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Agnes Thornhill was born in 1302, in Huddersfield, Yorkshire, England as the daughter of John Thornhill and Beatrice Talboner. She married John Dronsfield in 1318, in Silkstone, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1356, in West Bretton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 54.

Elle est décédée en 1356, à West Bretton, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 54 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Thornhillは1302年にイギリスヨークシュアイアハッドエルスフィールドでJohn ThornhillとBeatrice Talbonerの娘として生まれた。1318年にイギリスヨークシュアイアシルクストーンでJohn Dronsfieldと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1356年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアウエスト ブレットオンで54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Thornhill(은)는 1302년 영국 요르크실르 허더스필드에서 John Thornhill(와)과 Beatrice Talboner의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1318년 영국 요르크실르 시크스톤에서 John Dronsfield(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1356년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 웨스트 브레턴에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Dronsfield в 1318 г. в местности силкстоун, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь.
